hook.text: The open ocean is the largest habitat on Earth, yet one of the least productive per area.

buildup.text: Away from coasts, nutrients are scarce. Phytoplankton grow only where currents deliver food.

discovery.text: Despite low density, the sheer volume of open water supports massive total biomass.

twist.text: A tuna crossing the Pacific encounters vastly different conditions every hundred miles.

climax.text: Open-ocean species travel enormous distances, linking ecosystems thousands of miles apart.

punchline.text: Empty-looking water hides a connected world.

quiz.question: Why is the open ocean relatively low in productivity?
options: Nutrients are scarce away from coasts
